misconceptions.description: Assumes integral_1^infinity 1/x converges because 1/x -> 0; the p=1 tail actually diverges, so f -> 0 is not sufficient.
trigger_phrases: 1/x goes to zero so it's finite
the integrand vanishes so it converges
since the function goes to 0 the area is finite

misconceptions.description: Evaluates the antiderivative at infinity as if it were a number instead of taking a limit as t -> infinity.

misconceptions.display_name: Same p-rule at 0 and at infinity
misconceptions.description: Uses p>1 => converges near x=0; the rule reverses at a finite singularity, where p<1 converges.
trigger_phrases: p greater than 1 converges at zero too
